message:
chore(beads): add triage path normalization bug tracking issue

Add issue tracking the triage directory path normalization fix
that was implemented in the preceding commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-4uecx","title":"Feature: Robot verbosity profiles (terse|default|debug)","description":"## Overview
Add optional verbosity profiles that trade token cost for detail while keeping outputs machine‑parseable.

## Profiles (JSON/TOON only)
- **terse**: minimal fields, short keys, no hints
- **default**: current behavior
- **debug**: includes raw/trace data

## Interface
- `--robot-verbosity=terse|default|debug`
- Config: `robot.verbosity`

## Compatibility
- `--robot-terse` remains the **existing single‑line encoded state** output and is not changed by this feature.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Profiles adjust field inclusion deterministically
- JSON + TOON both supported
- Existing `--robot-terse` output remains unchanged","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-4uo5l","title":"Task: Audit trail for REST/WS actions","description":"## Scope\nRecord audit trail for all mutating REST actions and approval decisions.\n\n## Implementation\n- Record who/what/when/result/request_id for mutating calls.\n- Include session/pane/agent IDs where relevant.\n- Persist to durable log (SQLite/JSONL) with retention.\n\n## Logging\n- log/slog emits audit record IDs.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: audit records created for mutating endpoints.\n- Integration: approval actions create audit records.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Audit trail covers all mutating API calls.\n- Records are queryable for troubleshooting.\n","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:54:33.307619864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:38:41.844913369Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4uo5l","depends_on_id":"bd-3bwhg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-4uo5l","depends_on_id":"bd-3mwd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4xgr6","title":"Task: Implement TOON encoder for robot outputs","description":"## Overview
Implement TOON serialization for robot payloads following the chosen strategy (from bd-2csfi).

## Requirements
- Support uniform arrays of objects (tabular TOON)
- Preserve numeric/bool/null types
- Stable ordering of fields for deterministic output
- Handle nested objects/arrays (fall back to inline TOON blocks or JSON string if needed)
- Prefer tab delimiter for token efficiency when safe

## If CLI Strategy Chosen
- Call `toon` CLI with `--encode` and `--delimiter "\t"`
- Validate output and handle errors clearly
- Keep JSON fallback for `auto`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Encoder produces valid TOON for common payloads
- Deterministic ordering across runs
- Emits clear error when payload cannot be encoded","notes":"Strategy update: implement minimal in-process TOON encoder in Go; remove CLI wrapper path. Encode uniform arrays of objects + primitives; deterministic field ordering; tab delimiter only when safe; unsupported shapes => error or JSON fallback in auto.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:27.580112022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T07:29:12.925177649Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T07:29:12.925091417Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4xgr6","depends_on_id":"bd-1fmug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-4xgr6","depends_on_id":"bd-2csfi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-55pm","title":"Direct bv integration - ntm reads from bv --robot-triage without shell piping","description":"## Overview
Direct integration with bv (beads viewer) for graph-aware task prioritization without shell piping.

## Implementation
```go
type BVClient struct {
    WorkspacePath string
    CacheTTL      time.Duration  // Default 30s
    Timeout       time.Duration  // Default 10s
}

func (c *BVClient) GetRecommendations(opts RecommendationOpts) ([]Recommendation, error)
func (c *BVClient) GetInsights() (*Insights, error)
func (c *BVClient) IsAvailable() bool

type RecommendationOpts struct {
    Limit       int      // Max recommendations (default 20)
    Strategy    string   // balanced, speed, quality, dependency
    FilterReady bool     // Only actionable items
}

type Recommendation struct {
    ID             string   
    Title          string   
    Priority       int      
    PageRank       float64  
    Betweenness    float64  // Critical path importance
    UnblocksCount  int      
    UnblocksIDs    []string // What this unblocks
    BlockedByIDs   []string // What blocks this
    IsActionable   bool     // No blockers
    Tags           []string
    EstimatedSize  string   // small, medium, large
}

type Insights struct {
    Cycles      [][]string        // Dependency cycles (must fix!)
    Bottlenecks []BottleneckNode  // High betweenness nodes
    ReadyCount  int
    TotalCount  int
}
```

## Error Handling
- bv not installed: Return clear error with install instructions
- bv timeout (>10s): Return partial results if available, error otherwise
- bv workspace mismatch: Auto-detect workspace from session path
- bv returns cycles: Warn user, exclude cyclic beads from recommendations
- Empty recommendations: Return empty slice, not error

## Caching
- Cache recommendations for 30s (configurable)
- Invalidate on: bead status change, new bead created
- Cache key: workspace + strategy + limit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BVClient can call bv --robot-triage
- [ ] Returns typed Recommendation structs with ALL fields
- [ ] Handles bv not installed gracefully with clear message
- [ ] Handles bv timeout with partial results
- [ ] Supports --limit parameter
- [ ] Supports strategy filtering
- [ ] Caches results with configurable TTL
- [ ] Detects workspace from session path
- [ ] Warns about cycles in insights

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Parse all bv JSON fields correctly
- Unit test: Error handling (not found, timeout, invalid JSON)
- Unit test: Cache hit/miss/invalidation
- E2E test: Integration with real bv workspace","notes":"Prerequisite for ntm assign; keep as separate feature under assignment epic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:35:24.150568652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:52:18.329685962Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:52:18.329584561Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-55pm","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-56lze","title":"Feature: Robot ecosystem tool status (ntm --robot-tools)","description":"## Overview\nExpose a robot-first ecosystem report for all tool adapters.\n\n## Deliverables\n- New robot flag: --robot-tools (or --robot-ecosystem)\n- JSON includes: installed, version, capabilities, health, path, required/optional\n- Include in --robot-snapshot summary or provide separate endpoint\n- Reuse adapter registry + doctor logic\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Machine-readable JSON only\n- Works even when tools missing (healthy=false + reason)\n- Sub-1s for local checks","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-58w0z","title":"Parse and validate Robot Probe CLI flags","description":"Parse and validate CLI flags for Robot Probe command.

## Flags

| Flag | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `--method=<M>` | string | keystroke_echo | Probe method to use |
| `--timeout=<MS>` | int | 5000 | How long to wait for response (ms) |
| `--aggressive` | bool | false | Use interrupt_test if keystroke_echo fails |

## Validation

- method must be one of: keystroke_echo, interrupt_test
- timeout must be positive integer, 100-60000 range
- aggressive requires method to be keystroke_echo (or unset)

## Implementation

```go
type RobotProbeFlags struct {
    Method     string
    TimeoutMs  int
    Aggressive bool
}

func parseProbeFlags() (*RobotProbeFlags, error) {
    method := flag.String("method", "keystroke_echo", "Probe method")
    timeout := flag.Int("timeout", 5000, "Response timeout in ms")
    aggressive := flag.Bool("aggressive", false, "Fallback to interrupt_test")
    
    // Validation
    validMethods := []string{"keystroke_echo", "interrupt_test"}
    if !contains(validMethods, *method) {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid method: %s, must be one of %v", *method, validMethods)
    }
    
    if *timeout < 100 || *timeout > 60000 {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("timeout must be 100-60000ms, got %d", *timeout)
    }
    
    if *aggressive && *method != "keystroke_echo" {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("--aggressive only valid with --method=keystroke_echo")
    }
    
    return &RobotProbeFlags{...}, nil
}
```

## Error Output

Invalid flags should return structured error:
```json
{
  "error": true,
  "error_code": "INVALID_FLAGS",
  "message": "invalid method: foo, must be one of [keystroke_echo, interrupt_test]",
  "valid_methods": ["keystroke_echo", "interrupt_test"]
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:44:34.887573672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:38:05.100933769Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:38:05.100885048Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-58w0z","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
